_comment: Absolute CI bench ceilings (ADR-023). Deliberately generous — shared GitHub runners are noisy; this gate catches order-of-magnitude regressions, not single-digit-percent drift, which the reference-machine Criterion history tracks instead. Each entry records its derivation so recalibration is an evidence exercise, not archaeology. Analytical entries are recalibrated from the first reference run at M3 exit.

scene_graph::hit_test_miss/1000.basis: analytical (~1.3 µs, ADR-023 extrapolation) — recalibrate after first reference run

scene_graph::hit_test_miss/10000.basis: analytical (~13–30 µs, ADR-023 extrapolation) — recalibrate after first reference run

scene_graph::hit_test_miss/100000.basis: analytical (~0.3–0.6 ms, ADR-023 extrapolation) — recalibrate after first reference run

scene_graph::hit_test_top_10k.basis: analytical (first-probe hit, ~ns-scale) — recalibrate after first reference run

scene_graph::move_node_to_index_10k.basis: analytical (10k-id memmove, µs-scale) — recalibrate after first reference run
